channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Cape Verde’s remarkable rise and breakthrough performance at the FIFA World Cup 2026
item.description: Cape Verde, a small West African island nation with a population of just over half a million people, has emerged as one of the surprise success stories of the FIFA World Cup 2026. Despite its limited ...

item.title: Obituary: Dr. Michael Klein, physician and researcher who transformed maternity care by advocating for less intervention in childbirth
item.description: Dr. Michael Klein, a Canadian family physician, researcher and influential advocate for more natural childbirth practices, has died on June 10 in Roberts Creek, British Columbia. He was 86. Over a car...

item.title: Kakamega woman suspected of killing husband in milk dispute, family dispute and prior mediation revealed
item.description: A 59-year-old woman from Kakamega County is reportedly on the run after allegedly killing her 62-year-old husband following a domestic dispute involving milk. According to reports from local sources, ...

item.title: Tanzania Tourism Ministry launches discounted travel packages at Dar es Salaam International Trade Fair
item.description: The Ministry of Natural Resources and Tourism in Tanzania has introduced special discounted tourism packages at the 50th Dar es Salaam International Trade Fair (DITF), also known as Sabasaba. The init...

item.title: Venezuela earthquake rescue: survivor found after eight days amid rising death toll
item.description: In Venezuela, rescuers achieved a remarkable breakthrough when they pulled a 43-year-old man alive from the rubble of a collapsed building eight days after powerful twin earthquakes struck the country...

item.title: Attendee at political state fair uses baptismal pool for relief during extreme heatwave
item.description: An attendee at a political-themed state fair linked to President Donald Trump sought relief from extreme heat by entering a baptismal pool set up at a religious tent during a severe heatwave affecting...

item.title: Sixteen killed and dozens injured in N1 bus crash near Touwsriver
item.description: A serious road accident on the N1 near Touwsriver in the Western Cape has claimed the lives of sixteen people and left many others injured. According to the Road Traffic Management Corporation (RTMC),...

item.title: National gun buyback scheme stalls as states refuse to sign on by deadline
item.description: A federal deadline for states and territories to commit to a proposed national gun buyback scheme has expired, exposing deep divisions between jurisdictions and casting doubt over the rollout of the r...

item.title: Groups urge Supreme Court to decide BARMM districting petitions ahead of 2026 parliamentary elections
item.description: A group from the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM) has once again called on the Supreme Court to urgently resolve pending petitions related to the region’s first parliamentary el...

item.title: US policy shifts and Chinese battery dominance shape the rise of Slate’s low-cost electric truck
item.description: The article examines how changes in U.S. policy and global battery supply chains have influenced the development of Slate’s new low-cost electric vehicle. Slate, a Michigan-based startup, recently unv...

item.title: NBA Analyst David Thorpe Criticizes Portland Trail Blazers' Ja Morant Trade Over Defensive Concerns
item.description: NBA analyst and coach David Thorpe has strongly criticized the Portland Trail Blazers' recent trade that sent Jerami Grant and Kris Murray to the Memphis Grizzlies in exchange for point guard Ja Moran...

item.title: Fourth of July Events, Fireworks, Parades, and Activities in Washington D.C. This Weekend
item.description: Washington D.C. is gearing up for a festive long weekend centered around the Fourth of July celebrations. The article highlights numerous events and activities both on and off the National Mall, encou...

item.title: Federal government confirms continuation of B.C. North Coast oil tanker ban alongside new energy and infrastructure agreement
item.description: The federal government has confirmed it will fully maintain the North Coast oil tanker ban off the coast of northern British Columbia, according to Prime Minister Mark Carney and B.C. Premier David Eb...

item.title: House fire reported in West Triangle area of Quezon City
item.description: A house fire was reported in the West Triangle area of Quezon City, according to ABS-CBN News. The incident involved a residential property where flames reportedly broke out, prompting attention due t...

item.title: Lahore Tuition Centre Roof Collapse Claims 14 Lives Due to Weak Structure and Extra Load During Repairs
item.description: In a tragic incident in Lahore's Kahna area, the roof of a tuition centre collapsed, resulting in the sad death of 14 innocent children. Punjab police have termed this event as criminal negligence and...

item.title: Low-scoring NBC Premier League season delivers a tight title race in Tanzania
item.description: The 2025/26 NBC Premier League season in Tanzania was marked by a noticeable drop in goals among the top clubs, but it produced one of the most competitive title races in recent years. Young Africans ...

item.title: SpY’s Kinetic Installation Features Fifteen Illuminated Rings in Continuous Motion
item.description: Spanish urban artist SpY has created a large-scale kinetic installation featuring fifteen illuminated rings suspended within an interior space in Kazan, Russia. Each ring moves vertically in an indepe...

item.title: Lasaco Assurance settles N17.6bn insurance claims in 2025 financial year
item.description: Lasaco Assurance Plc has reported that it settled insurance claims worth N17.60 billion during its 2025 financial year, showing strong commitment to policyholders and reliability in claim payments. Th...

item.title: Siaya man builds KSh 950k mabati home with lush landscaped compound
item.description: Joel, a man from Uyoma in Siaya County, has attracted public attention after showcasing his creatively designed rural home developed on a budget of about KSh 950,000. He initially lived in a simba wit...

item.title: A care worker’s journey from Labour voter to participation in far-right marches and what it reveals about social change in Britain
item.description: The article explores the personal and political transformation of Sandra, a former care worker and long-time Labour voter who later became involved in marches organised by the English Defence League (...
